title: QA: HookEngine get_entries_for_event is a god switch coupling every event type
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
solid
## Location
src/hooks/mod.rs:456-481
## Violation
`get_entries_for_event` is a 25-line `match` returning `&[HookEntry]` for
each of 16 event variants:
```rust
match event {
    HookEvent::SessionStart => &self.config.session_start,
    HookEvent::SessionEnd => &self.config.session_end,
    HookEvent::PreToolUse => &self.config.pre_tool_use,
    ...
    HookEvent::VddConverged => &self.config.vdd_converged,
}
```
Adding a new `HookEvent` requires editing six places: the enum, the
`config_key` match (line 79), the `from_claude_code_name` match (line
102), the `HooksConfig` struct (one Vec<HookEntry> field), the
`HooksConfig::is_empty` check (line 129), and this dispatch match. SRP
violation (one reason to change becomes six) and OCP violation
(modification required for every new event). The fallback logic at lines
462-468 also conflates routing with policy.
## Mandated refactor
Replace `HooksConfig`'s 16 named fields with a
`HashMap<HookEvent, Vec<HookEntry>>`. `get_entries_for_event` becomes
`self.config.entries.get(&event).map(Vec::as_slice).unwrap_or(&[])`.
The PostToolUseFailure → PostToolUse fallback moves to a documented
`resolve_event(event)` function. `is_empty` becomes
`entries.values().all(Vec::is_empty)`. New events require only one
change: add the variant.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
